info.name: Qeet Identity API
info.description:
Complete request collection for the qeet-identity backend (Go monolith).

## Setup

1. Start the backend: `cd backend && make run` (defaults to `http://localhost:4001`).
2. Import this collection AND the companion environment (`qeet-identity.postman_environment.json`).
3. Select the **qeet-identity (local)** environment in the Postman environment dropdown.
4. **First time / fresh DB:** run **Auth → Signup**. It creates a new tenant + user + owner role in one shot and auto-populates `{{tenantId}}`, `{{userId}}`, `{{accessToken}}`, `{{refreshToken}}`. The signup user becomes the tenant admin.
5. **Returning users:** run **Auth → Login** with the saved tenant_id + email + password.

## Conventions

- All authed requests inherit a `Bearer {{accessToken}}` header from the collection root.
- IDs (`{{tenantId}}`, `{{userId}}`, `{{roleId}}`, …) are written back to collection variables by the corresponding `Create` / `Signup` requests, so you can chain them.
- Routes prefixed `/v1/...` are versioned API surface. Discovery / JWKS live at root (`/.well-known/...`) per the OIDC spec.

## Status legend

Routes marked `[501]` in the request name are stubbed in the current implementation — they return 501 Not Implemented. See `documents/IMPLEMENTATION-STATUS.md`.

exec: throw new Error('baseUrl is not set. Select the qeet-identity (local) environment or override --env-var baseUrl=...');
// The backend CSRF middleware (httpx/csrf.go) requires non-Bearer mutation
// requests to carry (a) an allow-listed Origin/Referer and (b) a double-
// submit cookie pair (qe_csrf + X-CSRF-Token). Bearer-token traffic skips
// entirely. Postman is not a browser so it sends neither header — handle
// it transparently here so the public auth routes (signup/login/recovery/
// invite-accept/oauth-token) work out of the box.
// If we already have a cached CSRF token from a prior seed, just echo it.
// Otherwise, seed the cookie by GETting /healthz and extract qe_csrf
// from Set-Cookie. Postman will wait for this async call before firing
pm.sendRequest({ url: baseUrl + '/healthz', method: 'GET' }, function (err, res) {
// Skip everything if the request errored out (no response, e.g. connection refused).
// Universal sanity checks. Per-request tests still run.
// Safety-net token capture: if any response carries access_token / refresh_token,
// keep collection vars in sync. Per-request tests still own the primary capture.
} catch (e) { /* non-JSON or empty body — ignore */ }
console.log('Signed up — you are now owner of tenant', body.tenant_id);
// Persist the email/password actually sent so Login can re-use them.

request.description:
Public self-service signup. Takes only `{email, password, display_name?}`. In ONE transaction the backend:

1. Generates a personal tenant for the new user (slug like `personal-<12hex>`, name like `"Jane's workspace"`).
2. Creates the user with a password credential.
3. Creates a system `owner` role for the personal tenant and grants it every platform permission.
4. Assigns the user to that owner role.
5. Issues an access + refresh token pair (auto-login).

The user can rename their workspace or create more from `Tenants → New tenant`.

**Email is globally unique** — same address can't sign up twice across the platform.

**Errors:**
- 409 — email already in use
- 422 — validation failed (password < 8 chars, invalid email, …)

item.name: Authed call with API key — GET /v1/users (ApiKey auth)

request.description: Verifies the tamper-evident hash chain across audit events for the tenant. Returns ok=true plus the verified range; ok=false with break_at when a tampered row is detected.
Returns rollup metrics for the admin dashboard: total users, MAU, signups vs prior period, signins, failure rates, weekly activity (last 8 weeks).
Inspects dead-lettered outbox events for ops triage. Requires platform-admin gating in the deployment.
